biography: I am Lecturer in International Development in the Department of Development Studies. Sitting in the overlap between human geography and anthropology, I bring practical and theoretical insights from political ecology and science and technology studies to a research agenda which encompasses environment and development in Southern Africa and South America. Broadly, my research fits into two areas: the relationship between conservation and development climate change adaptation and development
research_interests: As highlighted in my bio; my empirical research is most related to two areas: the relationship between conservation and development; and climate change adaptation and development. On the former; I wrote my Ph; D on privileged knowledge and the implications for local-level participation in combined conservation and development initiatives in Argentina and Namibia
